steps.title: Read the axe metaphor
steps.content: The underlined phrases compare the speakers' attempts to clarify their ideas about art to using an ill-balanced axe and making strokes that go wrong. The next sentence says they privately corrected what they had just said publicly.

steps.title: Separate metaphor from literal action
steps.content: Choice A invents puzzlement about an artwork's reputation. Choice B treats the axe imagery as literal construction of art. Choice C focuses on discontent with the conversation, but the imagery emphasizes difficulty expressing thoughts clearly. The answer is D.
